title: Package Authority Map v1
description: Schema mapping every known package in the Tribunus workspace to its canonical identity: current name, intended name, ownership class, and migration strategy. Serves as the single source of truth for package identity during the OpenCode->Tribunus cutover.

packages.description: All known packages in the workspace, including those pending migration, third-party dependencies, and obsolete packages.

currentName.description: Current package name as declared in its package.json, e.g. "@tribunus/sdk"

currentPath.description: Relative filesystem path to the package root, e.g. "packages/sdk/js"

intendedName.description: Target package name after full migration, e.g. "@tribunus/sdk"

intendedPath.description: Target filesystem path after migration (may be same as currentPath)

ownership.description: Ownership classification determining how the package is managed

isWorkspace.description: Whether the package is resolved as workspace:* in the monorepo

isPublished.description: Whether the package is published to a package registry

repositoryOfRecord.description: URL of the primary repository hosting this package

upstreamOrigin.description: URL of the original upstream source, if the package is forked, vendored, or derived

compatibilityRequirements.description: Details of required backward-compatibility guarantees (API surface, protocol, etc.)

externalConsumers.description: Known external consumers of this package's public API

migrationStrategy.description: The approach to migrate this package to its intended identity

removalCondition.description: Condition under which this package can be removed (e.g. after all consumers migrate)

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this authority map was generated
